title: Cancer biology and molecular genetics of A<sub>3</sub> adenosine receptor.
author_string: Mazziotta, Chiara, Rotondo, John Charles, Lanzillotti, Carmen, Campione, Giulia, Martini, Fernanda et al.
abstract: A<sub>3</sub> adenosine receptor (A<sub>3</sub>AR) is a cell membrane protein, which has been found to be overexpressed in a large number of cancer types. This receptor plays an important role in cancer by interacting with adenosine. Specifically, A<sub>3</sub>AR has a dual nature in different pathophysiological conditions, as it is expressed according to tissue type and stimulated by an adenosine dose-dependent manner. A<sub>3</sub>AR activation leads to tumor growth, cell proliferation and survival in some cases, while triggering cytostatic and apoptotic pathways in others. This review aims to describe the most relevant aspects of A<sub>3</sub>AR activation and its ligands whereas it summarizes A<sub>3</sub>AR activities in cancer. Progress in the field of A<sub>3</sub>AR modulators, with a potential therapeutic role in cancer treatment are reported, as well.
